questions.text: If A = {1, 2} and B = {a, b}, then A × B is:
questions.explanation: The Cartesian product A × B is the set of all ordered pairs (x, y) where x ∈ A and y ∈ B.

questions.text: If n(A) = p and n(B) = q, then n(A × B) is:
questions.explanation: The number of elements in the Cartesian product is the product of the number of elements in each set.

questions.text: A relation R from set A to set B is a subset of:
questions.explanation: A relation defines a correspondence between elements of A and B, represented by ordered pairs from A × B.

questions.text: The set of all first elements of the ordered pairs in a relation R from A to B is called the _____ of R.
questions.explanation: The domain consists of all possible input values.

questions.text: The set of all second elements of the ordered pairs in a relation R from A to B is called the _____ of R.
questions.explanation: The range consists of all actual output values.

questions.text: If R is a relation from A to B, then the entire set B is called the _____ of R.
questions.explanation: The codomain is the set of all possible output values, regardless of whether they are actually mapped to.

questions.text: A function f from A to B is a relation such that every element of A has _____ image in B.
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental definition of a function: each input has a unique output.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a type of function?
questions.explanation: A function cannot map one input to multiple outputs. If it does, it's a relation, not a function.

questions.text: If f(x) = x², then the domain of f is:
questions.explanation: The square function is defined for all real numbers.

questions.text: If f(x) = x², then the range of f is:
questions.explanation: The square of any real number is always non-negative.

questions.text: The identity function f(x) = x has a range of:
questions.explanation: For every real input, there is an equal real output.

questions.text: The graph of the modulus function f(x) = |x| is symmetric about the:
questions.explanation: f(x) = f(-x), which indicates symmetry about the y-axis.

questions.text: The domain of the function f(x) = 1/x is:
questions.explanation: Division by zero is undefined, so x cannot be 0.

questions.text: The range of the function f(x) = 1/x is:
questions.explanation: The reciprocal of any non-zero real number is also a non-zero real number.

questions.text: If f(x) = x + 7 and g(x) = x - 7, then (f o g)(x) is:
If f(x) = x² and g(x) = 2x + 1, then (f o g)(x) is:
A relation R on a set A is reflexive if for every x ∈ A, _____.
questions.explanation: Every element is related to itself.

questions.text: A relation R on a set A is symmetric if (x, y) ∈ R implies _____.
questions.explanation: If x is related to y, then y is related to x.

questions.text: A relation R on a set A is transitive if (x, y) ∈ R and (y, z) ∈ R implies _____.
questions.explanation: If x is related to y and y is related to z, then x is related to z.

questions.text: A relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive is called a/an _____ relation.
questions.explanation: Equivalence relations partition a set into disjoint equivalence classes.

questions.text: The domain of the square root function f(x) = √x is:
questions.explanation: The square root of a negative number is not a real number.

questions.text: The range of the square root function f(x) = √x is:
questions.explanation: The principal square root is always non-negative.

questions.text: If A = {1, 2, 3}, then the identity relation on A is:
questions.explanation: The identity relation relates each element to itself.

questions.text: The graph of a constant function f(x) = c is a:
questions.explanation: The output value remains constant regardless of the input.

questions.text: The domain of the function f(x) = √(x - 2) is:
questions.explanation: For the square root to be real, the expression inside must be non-negative.

questions.text: The range of the function f(x) = √(x - 2) is:
questions.explanation: The square root function always yields non-negative values.

questions.text: If f(x) = x² + 1, then f(-1) is:
A function f: A → B is one-one (injective) if:
options: Every element of B has a pre-image in A
Different elements of A have different images in B
Every element of A has a unique image in B
questions.explanation: No two distinct inputs map to the same output.

questions.text: A function f: A → B is onto (surjective) if:
options: Every element of B has a pre-image in A
Different elements of A have different images in B
Every element of A has a unique image in B
questions.explanation: The range of the function is equal to its codomain.

questions.text: If f(x) = 2x + 3, then f⁻¹(x) is:
questions.explanation: Let y = 2x + 3. Then x = (y - 3)/2. So f⁻¹(y) = (y - 3)/2.

questions.text: The domain of the function f(x) = |x| is:
questions.explanation: The modulus function is defined for all real numbers.

questions.text: The range of the function f(x) = |x| is:
questions.explanation: The absolute value of any real number is always non-negative.

questions.text: The greatest integer function f(x) = [x] has a range of:
questions.explanation: The output of the greatest integer function is always an integer.

questions.text: The signum function f(x) = {1 if x>0, 0 if x=0, -1 if x<0} has a range of:
questions.explanation: The function can only output these three specific values.

questions.text: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {a, b}, then the number of relations from A to B is:
questions.explanation: Number of relations = 2^(n(A) * n(B)) = 2^(3*2) = 2⁶.

questions.text: If f(x) = x³ and g(x) = x¹/³, then (f o g)(x) is:
questions.explanation: (f o g)(x) = f(g(x)) = f(x¹/³) = (x¹/³)³ = x.

questions.text: The domain of the function f(x) = 1/(x² - 4) is:
questions.explanation: The denominator cannot be zero, so x² - 4 ≠ 0, which means x ≠ 2 and x ≠ -2.

questions.text: The graph of f(x) = x³ is symmetric about the:
questions.explanation: f(-x) = (-x)³ = -x³ = -f(x), which indicates symmetry about the origin.

questions.text: Which of the following is a polynomial function?
questions.explanation: Polynomial functions involve only non-negative integer powers of x.

questions.text: If f(x) = x² + 2x + 1, then f(a + h) is:
questions.explanation: Substitute (a+h) for x: (a+h)² + 2(a+h) + 1 = a² + 2ah + h² + 2a + 2h + 1.
